skill.name: wiki-researcher
skill.description: Conducts multi-turn iterative deep research on specific topics within a codebase with zero tolerance for shallow analysis.
skill.summary: Perform deep, evidence-based codebase investigations through structured multi-turn analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 5 high-risk patterns and 1 medium-risk pattern. All findings are false positives caused by markdown syntax misinterpretation (table pipes, list dashes). The skill is a legitimate codebase research tool with no network requests, file writes, or credential access. It provides structured methodology for deep code analysis using standard read-only tools (Glob, Grep, Read).

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Syntax Misinterpreted
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at line 63. This is a false positive - the line contains '- NEVER repeat findings from prior iterations' with no backtick execution. The scanner incorrectly identified markdown syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 37, 49, 70. These are false positives - the lines contain markdown table formatting characters (|) and list markers (-), not cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'System reconnaissance' at line 65. This is a false positive - the line contains '- Include Mermaid diagrams...' with no reconnaissance activity.

content.user_title: Conduct Deep Codebase Research with Evidence-Based Analysis
content.value_statement: Shallow code reviews lead to misunderstandings and bugs. This skill enforces rigorous, evidence-based investigation of codebases through structured multi-turn analysis.
actual_capabilities: Traces actual code paths and function calls across multiple files
Distinguishes verified facts from inferences with confidence ratings
Maps architectural components and data flow through read-only tools
Identifies design patterns, technical debt, and coupling issues
Creates Mermaid diagrams grounded in real code implementations
Follows 5-iteration research process for comprehensive analysis
limitations: Only works with accessible source code files in the workspace
Cannot execute code or test runtime behavior
Requires manual invocation for each research topic
Analysis quality depends on codebase complexity and documentation

use_cases.title: Understand Legacy Codebases
use_cases.description: New team members rapidly learn complex systems by tracing actual code paths rather than relying on outdated documentation.

use_cases.title: Architectural Decision Analysis
use_cases.description: Evaluate design patterns, coupling, and technical debt before refactoring or adding new features.

use_cases.title: Bug Investigation
use_cases.description: Trace data flow and function calls to identify root causes of complex bugs spanning multiple modules.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: How does authentication work in this codebase?
prompt_templates.scenario: New developer needs to understand auth flow

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the data flow from API entry point to database storage for user creation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a refactor of user registration

prompt_templates.title: Pattern Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: What design patterns are used in the payment processing module? Identify trade-offs and risks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating technical debt in critical systems

prompt_templates.title: Deep System Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the complete request lifecycle from ingress through middleware, services, and data layers. Include all transformations, error handling, and external integrations.

output_examples.input: How does the caching layer work?
output_examples.output: HIGH Confidence: CacheManager (src/cache/manager.ts:15) implements LRU eviction. Cache entries flow through RedisCacheAdapter (src/cache/redis.ts:42) which calls Redis SETEX with TTL. Default TTL is 300s (config.ts:78). Cache miss fallback to DataLoader (src/dataloader/index.ts:12) for batch fetching.

MEDIUM Confidence: PaymentService.processPayment (src/services/payments.ts:89) wraps Stripe calls in try-catch. Failures log to Sentry (line 112) and emit PAYMENT_FAILED event (line 115). Event consumers: EmailService sends notification (src/email/handlers.ts:78), webhook dispatcher triggers external callbacks (src/webhook/dispatcher.ts:45). Have not verified retry logic.

best_practices: Provide specific topics or questions rather than generic 'analyze everything' requests
Use when you need depth rather than quick answers - this skill prioritizes thoroughness over speed
Combine with file navigation tools to jump to specific code locations mentioned in findings
Request Mermaid diagrams when visualizing complex architectures or data flows
anti_patterns: Avoid using for simple grep searches - use basic file search instead
Do not expect runtime behavior analysis - this skill traces static code paths only
Avoid asking about files outside the accessible workspace directory
Do not use for time-sensitive tasks - the 5-iteration process requires multiple tool calls

faq.question: How is this different from normal code analysis?
faq.answer: This skill enforces rigorous evidence standards. Every claim must cite file paths and line numbers. It uses 5 research iterations and explicitly distinguishes facts from inferences with confidence ratings.

faq.question: Can it execute code or test functionality?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads and analyzes source code. It cannot execute code, run tests, or observe runtime behavior.

faq.question: How long does analysis take?
faq.answer: The skill performs 5 iterative passes over the codebase. Complex topics may require multiple tool calls across dozens of files. Expect thorough analysis to take longer than casual exploration.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: Only read-only tools: Glob for file discovery, Grep for content search, and Read for file reading. It never writes files or executes commands.

faq.question: Will it work on any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill traces code paths and structure, not specific language syntax. It works with any codebase where files can be read as text.

faq.question: Can it find bugs or security issues?
faq.answer: It can identify suspicious patterns and logic flaws by tracing code paths. However, it is not a replacement for dedicated security scanners or dynamic analysis tools.
